perturbation.original_text: (h) Any enhancement developed by Vendor for the PMS Softwa re in reliance upon the Confidential Information shall (A) be provided in object code form only and (B) shall include commercially reasonable security protections in respect of any HCAI System authentication credentials that are stored by the PMS System between sessions. As between IBC and Vendor, any such HCAI System authentication credentials shall constitute Co nfidential Information of IBC an d may not be used in any way by Vendor or its personnel or contractors.
perturbation.changed_text: (h) Any enhancement developed by Vendor for the PMS Softwa re in reliance upon the Confidential Information shall (A) be provided in object code form only and (B) shall include commercially reasonable security protections in respect of any HCAI System authentication credentials that are stored by the PMS System between sessions. As between IBC and Vendor, any such HCAI System authentication credentials shall not constitute Confidential Information of IBC and may be used in any way by Vendor or its personnel or contractors.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original statement that HCAI System authentication credentials constitute Confidential Information of IBC and cannot be used by the Vendor. Now, the modified text states the credentials are not confidential and can be freely used by the Vendor which is creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Vendor shall: (i) Not use any Confidential Information except as required for the Purpose. For further certainty, the Purpose expressly excludes the development of or provision by Vendor of a gateway or any web-hosted service that consolidates the exchange of Claim Information between Providers and IBC or any web-based or application service provider ba sed practice management system. (ii) Not disclose any Confidential Information to third par ties, except as expressly provided in Sections 2(b) and 2(c) of this Agreement; (iii) Take reasonable security precautions, no less than those precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasona ble care, to keep confidential the Confidential Information; (iv) Refrain from disclosing, reproducing, summarizing and/or distributing C onfidential Information except as expressly required for the Purpose, and onl y as otherwise provided hereunder; and (v) Refrain from reverse engineering, decompiling or disa ssembling any software code disclosed by IBC to Vendor under the terms of this Agreement, except as expressly permitted by applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The changed text states that HCAI System authentication credentials do not constitute Confidential Information of IBC and may be used in any way by Vendor. However, the contradicted text states that Vendor shall not use any Confidential Information except as required for the Purpose. Because the changed text explicitly states that HCAI System authentication credentials are not confidential, this creates a direct conflict, as the contradicted section restricts the use of Confidential Information.

perturbation.original_text: (b) All Confidential Information is provided by IBC (i) on a non-exclusive basis and (ii) on an “as is” basis without any representation or warranty of any kind. An y use shall be solely at the risk of Vendor. Any software code included in the Confidential Information is for illustrative purposes only. IB C makes no warranties or representations with respect to the Confidential Information, including any wa rranties or representations that any Conf idential Information will be accurate, complete or up-to-date, or free of errors or omissions or that any Confidential Information w ill be fit for any purpose. Vendor is solely responsible for ensuring that the Confidential Informa tion is suitable for the Vendor’s needs. WITH RESPECT TO THE HCAI SYSTEM, THE SERVICES, OR ANY CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, IBC EXPRESSLY DISCLAIMS ANY WARRANTIES, REPRESENTATIONS OR CONDITIONS, IM PLIED OR EXPRESS, STAT UTORY OR OTHERWISE, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO ANY WARRANTY OR C ONDITION OF MERCHANTABIL ITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE OR NON-INFRINGEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) All Confidential Information is provided by IBC (i) on a non-exclusive basis and (ii) on a basis with full warranties of any kind. Any software code included in the Confidential Information is not for illustrative purposes only. IB C makes warranties or representations with respect to the Confidential Information, including any wa rranties or representations that any Conf idential Information will be accurate, complete or up-to-date, or free of errors or omissions or that any Confidential Information w ill be fit for any purpose. Vendor is not solely responsible for ensuring that the Confidential Informa tion is suitable for the Vendor’s needs. WITH RESPECT TO THE HCAI SYSTEM, THE SERVICES, OR ANY CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, IBC EXPRESSLY GUARANTEES ANY WARRANTIES, REPRESENTATIONS OR CONDITIONS, IM PLIED OR EXPRESS, STAT UTORY OR OTHERWISE, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO ANY WARRANTY OR C ONDITION OF MERCHANTABIL ITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE OR NON-INFRINGEMENT.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly disclaims all warranties related to the confidential information. This modification reverses that statement, claiming the IBC guarantees warranties about the accuracy and suitability of the Confidential Information. This is a clear contradiction of the original disclaimer and leads to uncertainty and in-text contradiction about the obligations of each party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) All Confidential Information is provided by IBC (i) on a non-exclusive basis and (ii) on a basis with full warranties of any kind. Any software code included in the Confidential Information is not for illustrative purposes only. IB C makes warranties or representations with respect to the Confidential Information, including any wa rranties or representations that any Conf idential Information will be accurate, complete or up-to-date, or free of errors or omissions or that any Confidential Information w ill be fit for any purpose. Vendor is not solely responsible for ensuring that the Confidential Informa tion is suitable for the Vendor’s needs. WITH RESPECT TO THE HCAI SYSTEM, THE SERVICES, OR ANY CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, IBC EXPRESSLY GUARANTEES ANY WARRANTIES, REPRESENTATIONS OR CONDITIONS, IM PLIED OR EXPRESS, STAT UTORY OR OTHERWISE, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO ANY WARRANTY OR C ONDITION OF MERCHANTABIL ITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE OR NON-INFRINGEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly disclaims all warranties, while the changed text explicitly guarantees warranties. The Changed Text also states that the software code included in the confidential information is not for illustrative purposes only, whereas previously it was. The changed text also states that Vendor is not solely responsible for ensuring that the Confidential Information is suitable for the Vendor’s needs. The contradiction is that the original text assigns sole responsibility to the vendor, while the changed text removes that sole responsibility. Since the Changed text modifies the original text to create an in-text contradiction according to the Perturbation Explanation, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: (h) IBC may terminate this Agreement with or without cause upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the Vendor. IBC will not exercise such discretion to terminate the Agre ement with a specific vendor except for commercially reasonable grounds.
perturbation.changed_text: (h) IBC may terminate this Agreement with or without cause and zero (0) days prior written notice to the Vendor. IBC will exercise such discretion to terminate the Agre ement with a specific vendor with no need for commercially reasonable grounds.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that IBC may terminate the agreement with or without cause but requires a 30-day written notice and is limited by commercially reasonable grounds. The modified text allows termination with no notice and removes the requirement for any reasonable cause, contradicting the provision for written notice and creating in-text contradiction and uncertainty about termination rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (h) IBC may terminate this Agreement with or without cause and zero (0) days prior written notice to the Vendor. IBC will exercise such discretion to terminate the Agre ement with a specific vendor with no need for commercially reasonable grounds. All sections of this Agreement relating to the rights and obligations of the parties concerning Confidential Informat ion disclosed during the term of the Agreement shall survive any such termination. Upon any termination of th is Agreement, Vendor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to, as promptly as is practical, remove any functionality incorporated in the PMS System that is based on the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 30-day notice requirement and the 'commercially reasonable grounds' limitation for termination, directly contradicting the original text's condition of such requirements. The contradicted text, being identical to the changed text, reinforces this contradiction, by stating IBC has no need for commercially reasonable grounds. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document concerning termination rights and conditions, resulting in an in-text contradiction. Therefore it adheres to the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.'

perturbation.original_text: 3. Remedies Vendor acknowledges that monetary damages would not be a sufficient remedy fo r unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information and that IBC shall be entitled, without waiving any ot her rights or remedies, to such injunctive or equitable relie f as may be deemed proper by a court of competent jurisdiction, without the necessity of proving actual damages or posting any bonds.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Remedies Vendor acknowledges that monetary damages would be a sufficient remedy fo r unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information and that IBC shall not be entitled, without waiving any ot her rights or remedies, to such injunctive or equitable relie f as may be deemed proper by a court of competent jurisdiction, with the necessity of proving actual damages or posting any bonds.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that monetary damages are not a sufficient remedy for unauthorized disclosure and that IBC is entitled to injunctive relief. The modified text now states that monetary damages are a sufficient remedy, and that IBC is not entitled to injunctive relief. This directly contradicts the original remedy and creates in-text contradiction and uncertainty regarding the recourse available to IBC in case of a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Remedies Vendor acknowledges that monetary damages would be a sufficient remedy fo r unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information and that IBC shall not be entitled, without waiving any ot her rights or remedies, to such injunctive or equitable relie f as may be deemed proper by a court of competent jurisdiction, with the necessity of proving actual damages or posting any bonds. Vendor agrees to defend any and all Claims that may be brought or alleged agains t IBC and/or its resp ective directors, officers, employees, agents or contractors, and indemnify and ho ld harmless IBC and its respectiv e directors, officers, employe es, agents and contractors, from and against any and all Claims or damages that may arise, by reason of any breach of this Agreement by Vendor, its personnel or contractors or from Ve ndor’s use of the Confidenti al Information including any deficiency in or other issue relating to th e use or distribution of that portion of th e PMS System that is developed using the Confidential Information. For any Claims that IBC chooses to defend, the Vendor sh all, to the extent requested by IBC, participate in and co-operate with and assist IBC in such defence a nd any related settlement negotiations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The first part states that 'monetary damages would be a sufficient remedy' while the second part states 'IBC shall not be entitled to such injunctive or equitable relief...with the necessity of proving actual damages or posting any bonds.' The contradiction is that it first claims monetary damages are enough, but then denies injunctive relief, implying monetary damages are the *only* relief, but then requires proving actual damages, creating an inconsistent scenario. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, creating a self-contradiction. The original text created a scenario where monetary damages were not enough, allowing for injunctive relief, making the changed and contradicted text a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (d) This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall not be modified except by a written agreement dated subseque nt to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties. None of the provisions of this Agreement sh all be deemed to have been waived by any act or acquiescence on the part of IBC, the Vendor, their agents, or employees, but only by an instrument in writing signed by an authorized employee of IBC and the Vendor. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall c onstitute a waiver of any other provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion. No employee, agent or representative of IBC is authorized to make any additional representation , warranty or covenant on behalf of IBC.
perturbation.changed_text: (d) This Agreement does not constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall be modified by oral agreement dated subseque nt to the date of this Agreement and agreed by both parties. The provisions of this Agreement sh all be deemed to have been waived by any act or acquiescence on the part of IBC, the Vendor, their agents, or employees. Waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall c onstitute a waiver of any other provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion. Any employee, agent or representative of IBC is authorized to make any additional representation , warranty or covenant on behalf of IBC.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that the agreement is the complete agreement and can only be modified by a written document. It also states that waivers must be in writing. The modified version claims the agreement is incomplete and can be modified orally. Now stating waivers can be implied through actions/omissions, and that any IBC representative can alter the contract. This leads to a clear contradiction and in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) This Agreement does not constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall be modified by oral agreement dated subseque nt to the date of this Agreement and agreed by both parties. The provisions of this Agreement sh all be deemed to have been waived by any act or acquiescence on the part of IBC, the Vendor, their agents, or employees. Waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall c onstitute a waiver of any other provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion. Any employee, agent or representative of IBC is authorized to make any additional representation , warranty or covenant on behalf of IBC.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself within the same section. It states the agreement both does and does not constitute the entire agreement, that it can and cannot be modified orally, and that waivers can and cannot be implied. It also reverses the authorization of IBC representatives, creating a clear and irreconcilable contradiction, as the Changed Text and Contradicted text are in the same location.
